<!-- Generated by the ZetaVPN deep-research workflow (10 parallel research agents + synthesis).
     Compiled 2026-07-03. Sources are listed at the end of the report. -->

# ZetaVPN — Research & Architecture Report

*Lead architect briefing • compiled 2026-07-03 • ten dossiers synthesized*

---

## 1. Executive Summary

ZetaVPN is a Zeta-branded, self-hosted, single-node VPN/proxy management panel that must (a) support **all** major modern proxy protocols, (b) install from GitHub with one command, and (c) ship a real web UI portal — going beyond the Telegram-bot-only and TUI-menu-only tools that dominate the reseller ecosystem.

The landscape splits cleanly into two families. **Go single-binary panels** (3x-ui, S-UI) embed a proxy core, store state in SQLite, edit the core's `config.json`, and install via `curl|bash` — ideal for a single node. **Python/FastAPI multi-node panels** (Marzban, Marzneshin, PasarGuard, Hiddify's Flask stack) split a control plane from remote node agents talking gRPC + mutual-TLS — powerful but heavy. No single proxy core covers everything: **Xray-core** owns VLESS-REALITY/XTLS-Vision and the widest client-compatibility surface, while **sing-box** natively serves the QUIC/camouflage generation (Hysteria2, TUIC, ShadowTLS, NaiveProxy, AnyTLS) that Xray lacks. The Indonesian/SEA "AutoScript" genre contributes a third pillar entirely outside both cores: an **SSH/tunnelling stack** (OpenSSH + Dropbear + stunnel + WS + SlowDNS + BadVPN + OpenVPN + WireGuard) with Linux-system-user accounts.

**The chosen shape for ZetaVPN** (and the shape the existing `F:\ZetaVPN\backend` codebase already implements) is: *3x-ui's single-node feature breadth + Marzban's cleaner user/subscription model + the AutoScript SSH stack*, delivered on a **FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + SQLite** backend that drives **two proxy cores (Xray + sing-box)** as independent systemd services, plus native SSH tooling — with a deliberately **no-npm-build** front end (static HTML + vanilla JS, optional CDN-vendored Alpine.js). Multi-node gRPC, resellers, and Telegram bots are explicitly deferred past MVP.

The single most important non-negotiable, learned from the CVE record: **do not run the panel or the cores as root.** The three real CVE classes (default credentials, insecure update fetching, and authenticated config-injection → root RCE) are all mitigated by least-privilege systemd units, secure-by-default install-time secret generation, and verified downloads. Every hardening default ships **on**.

---

## 2. The Landscape — What to Learn from Each

### 2.1 The 3x-ui lineage (`vaxilu/x-ui → alireza0/x-ui → MHSanaei/3x-ui`)

The reference implementation for the whole category. Lineage:

- **`vaxilu/x-ui`** — original (Chinese README), one-line installer, protocol baseline (vmess/vless/trojan/shadowsocks/dokodemo/socks/http). Now legacy.
- **`alireza0/x-ui`** — English fork that added date+traffic caps activated on first use, the Telegram bot, XTLS-Vision/REALITY, and DB import/export.
- **`MHSanaei/3x-ui`** — current, most feature-complete fork. GPL-3.0, Go + Gin + gorm, embedded Xray-core.

**Learn from it:** the storage model (Xray-facing config kept as **opaque JSON text columns** — `settings`, `streamSettings`, `sniffing` — assembled into a live `config.json`), the env-var contract (`XUI_*`), the security baseline (random URL base path + random port + random 32-byte cookie secret at install, TOTP 2FA, a login limiter of **5 failures / 5-min window / 15-min cooldown** keyed by `ip+username`), fail2ban-based IP limiting, and the three-format subscription server. 3x-ui recently migrated its **classic denormalized client model** (clients as a JSON array inside `inbounds.settings.clients[]`) to a **normalized v3 schema** (separate `clients` + `client_inbounds` join tables). **ZetaVPN should adopt the normalized model** — far easier to query per-client traffic/expiry and attach one client to many inbounds.

### 2.2 Marzban (Gozargah) — Python/FastAPI/React, multi-node

Protocols limited to VMess/VLESS/Trojan/Shadowsocks, but its **user-centric subscription abstraction is the best in class**: one *user* owns a subscription token and is granted access to a *set* of inbounds via "services"; the `/sub` endpoint aggregates all the user's inbound links into one subscription with multi-format output (`/sub/{token}/{client_type}` where `client_type ∈ {sing-box, clash-meta, clash, outline, v2ray, v2ray-json}`). Stateless **JWT (HS256)** auth with the signing secret generated once as `os.urandom(32).hex()` and stored in a DB row; revocation via `password_reset_at` vs token `iat`. **Learn from it:** the user/subscription model and the token scheme (ZetaVPN already uses HS256 → the JWT-subscription variant is a drop-in). Marzban's weaknesses to *avoid*: `ALLOWED_ORIGINS="*"` default, dashboard blocks bare-IP access (domain+TLS mandatory), and the whole gRPC node machinery ZetaVPN doesn't need.

### 2.3 Marzneshin / marznode / PasarGuard

Next-gen Marzban rewrite. `marznode` is a multi-core gRPC agent (Xray + Hysteria + sing-box) with mutual-TLS, `AUTH_GENERATION_ALGORITHM=xxh128`, on port 53042. **Key idea to borrow:** "**services**" as a clean grouping layer between users and raw inbounds. **Key thing to skip:** the entire panel↔node control plane — the single largest complexity source in the landscape, and worthless for a personal single-node build. Model the DB so a `Node` table *could* be added later; do not build node agents now. PasarGuard is the actively-maintained Marzban successor — track it as a modern reference.

### 2.4 S-UI (alireza0) — the sing-box analogue of 3x-ui

Go panel built on **sing-box** rather than Xray. Ships two systemd services (`s-ui` and `sing-box`), panel on `2095` (`/app/`), subscription on `2096` (`/sub/`), default `admin/admin` (a security anti-pattern to avoid). Proves the **two-core, two-service, config-regenerate-and-reload** pattern that ZetaVPN adopts. Its Vue frontend is a separate repo embedded into the Go binary — the exact npm-build burden ZetaVPN's no-build stack sidesteps.

### 2.5 Hiddify-Manager — most batteries-included, heaviest

Python/Flask orchestrating **both** Xray and sing-box, advertising 20+ protocol variants across Direct/CDN/Domain-Fronting categories, plus WARP, DoH, Cloudflare/CDN auto-config, auto-backup every 6h, smart routing, and dedicated client apps. Optimized for China/Russia/Iran. **Learn from it:** the ambition to run two cores and cover the full modern anti-censorship matrix. **Avoid:** exposing every transport permutation as UI surface — ship a small set of *curated templates* instead (VLESS-REALITY, VLESS-WS-TLS, Trojan-WS-TLS, SS-2022, Hysteria2, TUIC).

### 2.6 The AutoScript / "stupul" genre (Indonesian/SEA)

Single-command installers turning a fresh Debian/Ubuntu VPS into an all-in-one tunnelling server: OpenSSH + Dropbear (many ports), stunnel4 (TLS-wrap SSH/OpenVPN), SSH-over-WebSocket (`ws.py` / Go `ws-epro`), OpenVPN (TCP+UDP), WireGuard, SlowDNS/dnstt (UDP:53→5300), BadVPN udpgw (UDP-over-SSH for games), Squid, and an nginx+vnstat portal. **Accounts are Linux system users** (`useradd -M -N -s /bin/false -e <expiry>` + `chpasswd`), expiry enforced by cron, concurrent logins capped by an "autokill" script; the whole admin surface is a bash `menu`. Modern variants (FN-Rerechan02) add a **Bearer-token JSON REST API on :9000** reverse-proxied at `/vps`. **Learn from it:** the exact port matrix, config field names, and account-lifecycle logic — this maps ~1:1 onto ZetaVPN's SSH stack. **Critical warning:** `Arya-Blitar22/st-pusat`'s `*.sh` files (`setup.sh`, `stupul.sh`, etc.) are **not scripts — they are `shc`-compiled, stripped x86-64 ELF binaries** (identified by the `E: neither argv[0] nor $_ works.` signature). ZetaVPN must ship **plaintext, auditable** bash + systemd templates — never obfuscated blobs. Treat any external installer shipping a stripped ELF named `*.sh` as a supply-chain red flag.

### 2.7 sing-box vs Xray — the decisive comparison

| | **Xray-core** | **sing-box** |
|---|---|---|
| License / lang | MPL-2.0 / Go | Go, ~35.6k★, v1.13.14 (2026-06-25) |
| Core strength | VLESS-REALITY, XTLS-Vision, widest client compat, existing subs | Hysteria2, TUIC v5, ShadowTLS 1/2/3, NaiveProxy, AnyTLS, modern QUIC/BBR |
| Live user add/remove | **Yes** — gRPC HandlerService (no restart, no dropped connections) | **No** live handler; batch config regen + reload (brief reconnect) |
| Config | single JSON: `log/inbounds/outbounds/routing/policy/dns` | single JSON: `log/dns/ntp/certificate/endpoints/inbounds/outbounds/route/services/experimental` |
| Control API | local gRPC StatsService/HandlerService | Clash-compatible RESTful `clash_api` (bearer secret) |

**Verdict: run both as separate systemd services, each owning its own JSON config on non-overlapping ports.** Use Xray for VLESS-REALITY/Vision + max compatibility; use sing-box for Hysteria2/TUIC/ShadowTLS/AnyTLS/Naive. The panel writes each config, validates (`xray run -test` / `sing-box check`), then restarts only the affected service.

---

## 3. Protocol Matrix

Legend for "core": **X** = Xray-core, **S** = sing-box, **SSH** = native tunnelling stack. Where both cores can serve a protocol, the recommended owner is bold.

| Protocol | Core(s) | Transports / security | When to use |
|---|---|---|---|
| **VLESS + REALITY + XTLS-Vision** | **X**, S | `raw`(tcp)/`xhttp`/`grpc`; security `reality`; `flow=xtls-rprx-vision` (raw only) | **Default one-click.** Best modern anti-censorship: masquerades as a real TLS site, no cert needed. |
| VLESS + WS/HTTPUpgrade/XHTTP + TLS | **X**, S | `ws`/`httpupgrade`/`xhttp`; `tls` | CDN-fronted (Cloudflare). Xray listens on `127.0.0.1`, nginx terminates TLS. |
| VLESS + gRPC + TLS/REALITY | **X**, S | `grpc` (serviceName, `h2` alpn first) | CDN/gRPC-friendly networks; multiplexing. |
| VMess + WS + TLS | **X**, S | `ws`/`grpc`/`xhttp`; `tls` | Legacy client compatibility. Requires host NTP sync (120s skew). |
| Trojan + TLS | **X**, S | `tcp`/`ws`/`grpc`; `tls`; supports fallbacks | Simple, TLS-native, looks like HTTPS. |
| Shadowsocks-2022 | **X**, S | `tcp,udp`; ciphers `2022-blake3-aes-128-gcm`(16B)/`-aes-256-gcm`(32B)/`-chacha20-poly1305`(32B) | Fast, PSK-based, no TLS overhead. Multi-user via server PSK + per-user PSK. |
| Shadowsocks (legacy AEAD) | **X**, S | `aes-256-gcm`, `chacha20-ietf-poly1305` | Old client compat only; prefer 2022. |
| **Hysteria2** | **S** (X has partial) | QUIC/UDP; `tls` required; salamander obfs; up/down mbps; masquerade | High-loss / high-latency links; aggressive BBR. Needs UDP/443 open. |
| **TUIC v5** | **S** | QUIC/UDP; `tls` required; congestion `bbr/cubic/new_reno` | QUIC alternative to Hysteria2; low overhead. |
| **ShadowTLS v1/2/3** | **S** | TLS camouflage front for a hidden SS inbound (via `detour`) | Disguise SS traffic as a TLS handshake to a real high-rep host. |
| **AnyTLS** | **S** (≥1.12) | traffic-padding over TLS; `padding_scheme` | Newest anti-analysis padding; absent from Xray. |
| **NaiveProxy** | **S** | HTTP/2 CONNECT + TLS + padding | Mimics normal browser HTTPS. |
| WireGuard | **X** (userspace) or kernel `wg-quick` | UDP; peers + allowedIPs | Fast full-tunnel VPN; kernel WG for the SSH-stack side. |
| SOCKS / HTTP / Mixed | X, S | plain (LAN/local only) | Local proxying, chaining; never expose unencrypted. |
| Tunnel (dokodemo-door) | X | tcp/udp port-map / tproxy | Port forwarding / transparent proxy. |
| MTProto | X (via `mtg` sidecar, FakeTLS) | — | Telegram proxy (later). |
| **SSH (WS / SSL / direct)** | **SSH** | OpenSSH:22 + Dropbear (442/109/…) + stunnel(444/445) + WS(80/8880/8443) | The SEA reseller staple. Rides HTTP/CDN via "bug host". |
| SSH-UDP custom | SSH | `udp-custom` (`:36712`) | UDP games over SSH accounts. |
| SlowDNS (dnstt) | SSH | UDP 53 → 5300 NAT | Last-resort tunnel where only DNS egress works. |
| BadVPN udpgw | SSH | `127.0.0.1:7300` (range 7100–7900) | UDP-over-SSH for games/VoIP. |
| OpenVPN | SSH | TCP 1194 / UDP 2200 (+ over-stunnel/WS/SlowDNS) | Classic VPN; `.ovpn` export. |

**Transport notes (Xray field renames — emit new names, accept old):** `tcp→raw`, `splithttp→xhttp`, REALITY `dest→target`, REALITY client `publicKey→password`. **QUIC transport was removed** from Xray — superseded by XHTTP (use `alpn:["h3"]` for HTTP/3). Keep mKCP only for latency-sensitive UDP-simulation. `xhttp` is Xray's recommended modern transport replacing WS/gRPC/SplitHTTP/QUIC.

---

## 4. Reference Config Schemas

### 4.1 Xray — VLESS + REALITY + Vision (the default template)

```json
{
  "port": 443,
  "protocol": "vless",
  "settings": {
    "clients": [{ "id": "<uuid>", "flow": "xtls-rprx-vision", "email": "user1" }],
    "decryption": "none"
  },
  "streamSettings": {
    "network": "tcp",
    "security": "reality",
    "realitySettings": {
      "show": false,
      "target": "www.yahoo.com:443",
      "xver": 0,
      "serverNames": ["www.yahoo.com", "news.yahoo.com"],
      "privateKey": "<x25519-priv>",
      "shortIds": ["", "0123456789abcdef"]
    }
  },
  "sniffing": { "enabled": true, "destOverride": ["http","tls","quic"], "routeOnly": true }
}
```
Keys via `xray x25519` (→ `privateKey` server / `password` client). `shortIds`: even-length hex, ≤16 chars, allow one empty. `xray tls ping <target>` to validate the SNI reaches a TLS1.3+H2 site.

### 4.2 Xray — VLESS + WS + TLS (CDN-fronted)

```json
{
  "port": 1234, "listen": "127.0.0.1", "protocol": "vless",
  "settings": { "clients": [{ "id": "<uuid>", "email": "user1" }], "decryption": "none" },
  "streamSettings": {
    "network": "ws", "security": "tls",
    "wsSettings": { "path": "/Path2WS", "host": "example.com" },
    "tlsSettings": {
      "serverName": "example.com", "alpn": ["h2","http/1.1"],
      "certificates": [{ "certificateFile": "/etc/ssl/fullchain.pem", "keyFile": "/etc/ssl/privkey.pem" }]
    }
  },
  "sniffing": { "enabled": true, "destOverride": ["http","tls"] }
}
```
Behind nginx, `security` is usually omitted (nginx terminates TLS; Xray binds loopback).

### 4.3 Xray — VMess + WS + TLS

```json
{
  "listen": "0.0.0.0", "port": 1234, "protocol": "vmess",
  "settings": { "clients": [{ "id": "<uuid>", "level": 0, "email": "user1" }] },
  "streamSettings": {
    "network": "ws", "security": "tls",
    "wsSettings": { "path": "/" },
    "tlsSettings": { "certificates": [{ "certificateFile": "/path/certificate.crt", "keyFile": "/path/key.key" }] }
  }
}
```
VMess requires host clock within 120s of real UTC → installer must ensure chrony/ntp.

### 4.4 Xray — Trojan + TCP + TLS

```json
{
  "port": 443, "protocol": "trojan",
  "settings": {
    "clients": [{ "password": "<pw>", "email": "love@example.com" }],
    "fallbacks": [{ "dest": 80 }]
  },
  "streamSettings": {
    "network": "tcp", "security": "tls",
    "tlsSettings": {
      "alpn": ["http/1.1"],
      "certificates": [{ "certificateFile": "/path/fullchain.crt", "keyFile": "/path/private.key" }]
    }
  }
}
```

### 4.5 Xray — Shadowsocks-2022 (multi-user)

```json
{
  "port": 1234, "protocol": "shadowsocks",
  "settings": {
    "method": "2022-blake3-aes-128-gcm",
    "password": "<server-psk-base64-16B>",
    "clients": [{ "password": "<user-psk-base64>", "email": "user1" }],
    "network": "tcp,udp"
  }
}
```
Client connects with `password = "<serverPsk>:<userPsk>"`. PSK via `openssl rand -base64 16` (128-gcm) or `32` (256-gcm / chacha20). **Validate PSK length against the cipher before writing config** or the core refuses to start.

### 4.6 sing-box — Hysteria2

```json
{
  "type": "hysteria2", "tag": "hy2-in", "listen": "::", "listen_port": 8443,
  "up_mbps": 100, "down_mbps": 100,
  "obfs": { "type": "salamander", "password": "cry_me_a_r1ver" },
  "users": [{ "name": "user1", "password": "goofy_ahh_password" }],
  "ignore_client_bandwidth": false,
  "masquerade": "https://news.ycombinator.com",
  "tls": { "enabled": true, "alpn": ["h3"], "certificate_path": "/etc/sing-box/cert.pem", "key_path": "/etc/sing-box/key.pem" }
}
```

### 4.7 sing-box — TUIC v5

```json
{
  "type": "tuic", "tag": "tuic-in", "listen": "::", "listen_port": 8444,
  "users": [{ "name": "sekai", "uuid": "059032A9-7D40-4A96-9BB1-36823D848068", "password": "hello" }],
  "congestion_control": "bbr", "auth_timeout": "3s", "zero_rtt_handshake": false, "heartbeat": "10s",
  "tls": { "enabled": true, "alpn": ["h3"], "certificate_path": "/etc/sing-box/cert.pem", "key_path": "/etc/sing-box/key.pem" }
}
```
Keep `zero_rtt_handshake:false` (replay-vulnerable). Every QUIC/camouflage sing-box inbound needs a working `tls` block (ACME or self-signed) unless REALITY is used.

### 4.8 sing-box — ShadowTLS v3 (chained in front of hidden Shadowsocks)

```json
{ "type": "shadowtls", "tag": "st-in", "listen": "::", "listen_port": 443, "version": 3,
  "users": [{ "name": "sekai", "password": "8JCsPssfgS8tiRwiMlhARg==" }],
  "handshake": { "server": "google.com", "server_port": 443 },
  "strict_mode": true, "detour": "ss-in" }
```
`ss-in` is a Shadowsocks inbound on an internal (non-public) `listen_port`. Expose as a single "ShadowTLS+SS" product in the UI.

---

## 5. SSH / Tunnelling Stack & Account Model

ZetaVPN's third pillar (native, no proxy core). Ship each component as a **systemd unit** (not `screen`+`rc.local` as the legacy scripts do) for reliability.

### 5.1 Components & default ports

| Component | Role | Ports / config |
|---|---|---|
| **OpenSSH** | interactive-less tunnel auth | 22 |
| **Dropbear** | lightweight SSH, extra ports | `/etc/default/dropbear`: `DROPBEAR_PORT=442`, `DROPBEAR_EXTRA_ARGS="-p 555"`, `DROPBEAR_BANNER="/etc/issue.net"` |
| **stunnel4** | TLS-wrap Dropbear/OpenVPN | `[dropbear] accept=444 connect=127.0.0.1:442`; `[openvpn] accept=441 connect=127.0.0.1:110`; `cert=/etc/stunnel/stunnel.pem` |
| **SSH-over-WS** | ride HTTP/CDN | Go `ws-epro` YAML: `listen_port: 8080 → target 127.0.0.1:22`, `http_response: 'HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols'`. Public ports 80/8880/8443 |
| **BadVPN udpgw** | UDP-over-SSH (games) | `badvpn-udpgw --listen-addr 127.0.0.1:7300 --max-clients 1000` (range 7100–7900) |
| **SlowDNS (dnstt)** | DNS tunnel | `dns-server -udp :5300 -privkey-file /root/server.key <NS.domain> 127.0.0.1:22`; `iptables -t nat -I PREROUTING -p udp --dport 53 -j REDIRECT --to-ports 5300` |
| **OpenVPN** | classic VPN | `openvpn-server@server-tcp-1194`, `openvpn-server@server-udp-2200`; `.ovpn` export |
| **WireGuard** | fast full tunnel | `wg genkey`/`wg pubkey`; `wg0.conf` `[Interface] Address=10.66.66.1/24 ListenPort=<port>`; `systemctl enable --now wg-quick@wg0` |
| **Squid** | HTTP proxy → localhost SSH | `acl SSH dst <serverip>`; `http_port 3128/8000/8080` |
| **UDP-custom** | UDP games | `{"listen": ":36712", "auth": {"mode": "passwords"}}` |

### 5.2 Account lifecycle (DB is source of truth, reconciled to OS)

Create:
```bash
useradd -M -N -s /bin/false -e "$(date -d "$days days" +%Y-%m-%d)" "$USER"
echo "$USER:$PASS" | chpasswd
```
`-M` no home, `-N` no group, `-s /bin/false` tunnel-only. Delete: `userdel`. Lock/unlock: `passwd -l/-u`. **Enforce expiry twice**: kernel via `useradd -e` *and* a ZetaVPN systemd-timer sweep that `userdel`s expired accounts and flips DB status (the `/etc/cron.d/user-expire` pattern, centralized). **Concurrent-login limit** ("autokill"): a periodic job counts `who`/`ps` sessions per user against `max_login` and kills excess PIDs. The existing `SSHAccount` model (`username`, `password`, `max_login`, `expiry_date`, `enabled`) already carries the needed fields.

### 5.3 The "bug host" / CDN bypass

Client sends a fake HTTP/WebSocket upgrade with a chosen `Host`/SNI (a Cloudflare-fronted domain); the WS proxy answers `101` and pipes to local SSH. The ISP sees only a connection to Cloudflare. ZetaVPN should let admins configure per-inbound WS path/Host + the 101 response string + backend `host:port`, and set nginx `real_ip_header CF-Connecting-IP` + Cloudflare CIDRs so logging/limits survive the CDN. Prefer the declarative `ws-epro` YAML over ad-hoc `ws.py`.

---

## 6. Subscription & Client Export

### 6.1 The four canonical URI schemes (+ two newer)

`vless://`, `trojan://`, `ss://`, `hysteria2://`, `tuic://` are `userinfo@host:port?query#remark` (percent-encoded fragment = display name). **VMess is the exception**: `vmess://` + base64(JSON). The de-facto reference for query params is v2rayN 7.23.1's `BaseFmt`. ZetaVPN already implements all six builders in `F:\ZetaVPN\backend\zeta\core\links.py` (`_vless/_vmess/_trojan/_shadowsocks/_hysteria2/_tuic`, `_stream_query()`, `subscription_for()`, `qr_data_url()`).

Shared query keys: `type` (network; `raw`==tcp), `security`, `sni`, `fp`, `pbk`, `sid`, `spx`, `flow`, `host`, `path`, `serviceName`, `mode`, `headerType`, `alpn`, `encryption`.

**Examples:**
```
vless://<uuid>@example.com:443?type=tcp&security=reality&sni=www.microsoft.com&fp=chrome&pbk=<x25519pub>&sid=6ba85179e30d4fc2&spx=%2F&flow=xtls-rprx-vision&encryption=none#Zeta-reality-user

vless://<uuid>@example.com:443?type=ws&security=tls&sni=example.com&fp=chrome&host=example.com&path=%2Fzetapath&encryption=none#Zeta-ws-user

trojan://password123@example.com:443?type=grpc&security=tls&sni=example.com&fp=chrome&serviceName=zeta&mode=gun#Zeta-trojan-user

ss://<base64url(method:password)>@example.com:8388#Zeta-ss-user
# 2022 cipher userinfo = base64url("method:serverPSK:userPSK")
```
VMess JSON field set: `v/ps/add/port/id/aid/scy/net/type/host/path/tls/sni/alpn/fp` (`aid` and `port` as strings; `net` stays `tcp`, not the internal `raw` alias).

### 6.2 Three aggregate formats, chosen by User-Agent

1. **Plain base64** — `base64("vless://…\nvmess://…")`, newline-joined. Universal (v2rayN/v2rayNG/NekoBox/Streisand). *ZetaVPN ships this today.*
2. **Clash/Clash.Meta (Mihomo) YAML** — `proxies:` list, native field names (`reality-opts:{public-key, short-id}`, `client-fingerprint`, `ws-opts:{path, headers:{Host}}`, `grpc-opts:{grpc-service-name}`, `packet-encoding: xudp`). UA contains `clash`/`mihomo`/`stash`. **← gap to build.**
3. **sing-box JSON** — `outbounds` array, snake_case, Reality under `tls.reality.public_key/short_id` + `tls.utls.fingerprint`; transports under `transport:{type:ws|grpc|http|httpupgrade, …, early_data_header_name:"Sec-WebSocket-Protocol"}`. UA contains `sing-box`/`SFA`/`SFI`/`hiddify`. **← gap to build.**

Keep **one canonical internal model** (`Inbound.stream_settings` + `Client`) and write **three renderers** — field names differ, so don't reuse the URI query dict for YAML/JSON. Add a `?target=clash|singbox|base64` override for testing.

### 6.3 Quota / expiry header + companions

```
Subscription-Userinfo: upload=1234567; download=89012345; total=107374182400; expire=1735689600
```
`expire` is **Unix seconds** (0 = never); `total=0` = unlimited. ZetaVPN stores `expiry_time` in **ms** and correctly divides by 1000 in `_userinfo_header`. Companions: `Profile-Update-Interval` (hours), `Profile-Title`, `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="Zeta-<subid>"`. Optionally `Profile-Web-Page-Url` for branded portals. *ZetaVPN emits these in `api/subscription.py`.*

### 6.4 QR

QR encodes the **single-config URI** string (not the subscription), error-correction level M. ZetaVPN uses the python `qrcode` lib → `data:image/png;base64` in `qr_data_url()`. For the user portal, also offer a QR of the *subscription URL itself* so users import the whole sub by scanning. Vendor `qrcodejs` locally rather than CDN.

---

## 7. Install Automation & VPS Hardening

### 7.1 `install.sh` structure (model on Xray-install + 3x-ui)

`set -euo pipefail` → root check → `/etc/os-release` distro detection (debian/ubuntu/armbian) → `arch()` mapping `uname -m` to Go suffixes (`amd64/arm64/armv7/386/s390x`) → resolve latest GitHub tag → **verified** download → install to `/usr/local/bin` → render systemd units (heredoc, idempotent overwrite) → `daemon-reload` + `enable --now`. Non-interactive mode auto-triggers when stdin is not a TTY (`[ ! -t 0 ]`) for `curl|bash`/cloud-init, replacing all prompts with `ZETA_*` env vars. Support flags `install|update|remove|--version X|--force`.

**Arch detection (Go style):** `x86_64|amd64→amd64; aarch64|arm64→arm64; armv7*→armv7; i*86→386; s390x→s390x`.

**Latest-tag lookup** (survive API rate limits with redirect fallback):
```bash
tag=$(curl -sSfL https://api.github.com/repos/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest | grep tag_name | awk -F'"' '{print $4}')
# fallback:
tag=$(curl -sLI https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/releases/latest | grep -i '^location:' | sed 's#.*/tag/##' | tr -d '\r')
```

**Verified download** (never `--no-check-certificate` — that was CVE-2025-29331):
```bash
curl -fLR --retry 5 --retry-delay 10 -o "$ZIP" "$URL"
curl -fsSR -o "$ZIP.dgst" "$URL.dgst"
CHECKSUM=$(awk -F '= ' '/256=/ {print $2}' "$ZIP.dgst")
[ "$CHECKSUM" = "$(sha256sum "$ZIP" | awk '{print $1}')" ] || exit 1
```

Base deps (Debian/Ubuntu): `apt-get install -y cron curl tar tzdata socat ca-certificates openssl` (socat needed for acme.sh standalone).

### 7.2 systemd (least-privilege — the CVE-2026-55477 mitigation)

Run cores as a **non-root** user with only the caps they need:
```ini
[Service]
User=zetavpn
Group=zetavpn
AmbientCapabilities=CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE      # + CAP_NET_ADMIN only if using tun
CapabilityBoundingSet=CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE
NoNewPrivileges=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=true
PrivateTmp=true
ReadWritePaths=/etc/zetavpn /var/log/zetavpn
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/xray run -config /usr/local/etc/xray/config.json
Restart=on-failure
LimitNOFILE=1000000
RuntimeDirectory=zetavpn
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
Use a template unit `zeta-core@.service` (`-config …/%i.json`) so the panel spawns multiple instances. Always `systemctl daemon-reload` after writing.

### 7.3 nginx + WS + TLS

```nginx
map $http_upgrade $connection_upgrade { default upgrade; '' close; }   # in http{}

server {
  listen 443 ssl; http2 on; server_name vpn.example.com;
  ssl_certificate     /root/cert/vpn.example.com/fullchain.pem;
  ssl_certificate_key /root/cert/vpn.example.com/privkey.pem;
  ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
  location /ws-path {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:10000;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection $connection_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_read_timeout 300s;
  }
}
# port-80 server: 301 to https, but keep /.well-known/acme-challenge/ on webroot
```
The `Upgrade/Connection` map is mandatory — nginx won't tunnel WS without it; raise `proxy_read_timeout` to keep long-lived tunnels alive.

### 7.4 acme.sh

```bash
curl https://get.acme.sh | sh -s email=admin@example.com
~/.acme.sh/acme.sh --set-default-ca --server letsencrypt      # ZeroSSL is default; LE is opt-in
# standalone (needs :80 + socat):
~/.acme.sh/acme.sh --issue -d example.com --standalone --httpport 80
# DNS-01 wildcard (no open port):
export CF_Token=xxx CF_Account_ID=xxx
~/.acme.sh/acme.sh --issue -d example.com -d '*.example.com' --dns dns_cf --server letsencrypt
# deploy + reload hook:
~/.acme.sh/acme.sh --install-cert -d example.com \
  --key-file /root/cert/example.com/privkey.pem \
  --fullchain-file /root/cert/example.com/fullchain.pem \
  --reloadcmd "systemctl reload nginx; systemctl restart zeta-xray zeta-singbox"
```
acme.sh's own cron handles renewal.

### 7.5 BBR + sysctl (`/etc/sysctl.d/99-zeta.conf`, apply `sysctl --system`)

```
net.core.default_qdisc = fq
net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control = bbr
net.ipv4.tcp_fastopen = 3
net.ipv4.tcp_mtu_probing = 1
net.core.somaxconn = 8192
net.ipv4.tcp_max_syn_backlog = 8192
net.core.netdev_max_backlog = 16384
net.core.rmem_max = 16777216
net.core.wmem_max = 16777216
net.ipv4.tcp_rmem = 4096 1048576 2097152
net.ipv4.tcp_wmem = 4096 65536 16777216
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1
```
`echo tcp_bbr > /etc/modules-load.d/bbr.conf; modprobe tcp_bbr`. Verify `sysctl net.ipv4.tcp_congestion_control` = bbr; warn on kernels <4.9.

### 7.6 Firewall + fail2ban + unattended-upgrades

```bash
ufw default deny incoming; ufw default allow outgoing
ufw limit 22/tcp; ufw allow 80/tcp; ufw allow 443/tcp; ufw allow 443/udp   # 443/udp for Hysteria2/TUIC
ufw --force enable
```
`fail2ban` (`/etc/fail2ban/jail.local`, never edit `jail.conf`): `[DEFAULT] bantime=1h findtime=10m maxretry=5 banaction=ufw` + `[sshd] enabled=true`. `unattended-upgrades` for security patches (`20auto-upgrades` + `50unattended-upgrades`).

### 7.7 Self-update

`zeta update`: resolve latest GitHub release → download+verify tarball → swap binary/panel while **preserving DB + config** → `systemctl restart` → check `is-active`, offer rollback. Thin delegate: `bash <(curl -Ls https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OWNER/REPO/main/update.sh)`. Pin `ZETA_XRAY_VERSION`/`ZETA_SINGBOX_VERSION` env vars for reproducible installs/rollbacks (Xray-core v26.3.27, sing-box v1.13.14 at time of research).

---

## 8. Security Best Practices & Known CVEs

### 8.1 The three real CVE classes (all avoidable by default)

| CVE | Root cause | ZetaVPN mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| **CVE-2026-55477** (3X-UI <3.3.1) | Authenticated admin abuses DB import to rewrite Xray log/config **file paths** → arbitrary file write → **RCE as root** (Xray runs as root) | **Don't run cores as root.** Allowlist/validate every field that becomes a path/port/command; render configs from safe templates, never string concat; sandbox the config-generator's write access; gate DB-import behind super-admin + confirmation with the same allowlists as live config. |
| **CVE-2025-29331** (3x-ui <2.5.3) | Update script passed `--no-check-certificate` to wget → MITM → RCE | Never disable TLS verification on downloads; verify SHA-256/signature; pin to the official GitHub org over HTTPS. |
| **CVE-2026-25803** (3DP-MANAGER ≤2.0.1) | Hardcoded default `admin/admin` | Generate random creds at install; track a `hasDefaultCredential` flag; force password change on first login; refuse to start with blank/known creds. |

### 8.2 Secure-by-default checklist (ship all ON)

- **No root.** Dedicated `zetavpn` system user; `CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE` only (or high port + reverse proxy); `NoNewPrivileges/ProtectSystem=strict/PrivateTmp`. `NET_ADMIN` scoped to the fail2ban/firewall component only.
- **Install-time secrets:** random admin username (≥8), password (≥16), 18+ char secret web base path (`XUI_INIT_WEB_BASE_PATH` analog), random high panel port, random JWT/cookie secret (`os.urandom(32).hex()` / `secrets.token_urlsafe(48)` — already in `config.py`). Write to a `install-result.env` (0600).
- **Auth:** session cookies for the browser (HttpOnly + Secure-when-TLS + SameSite=Lax, server-side store, a **login-epoch/version bumped on password change** to invalidate all sessions) *or* the HS256 JWT ZetaVPN already uses — but with short expiries (≤60 min access, revoke via `password_reset_at`/token-version; never accept `exp=0`). Separate high-entropy API bearer tokens hashed with **SHA-256** at rest (fast is fine — they're high-entropy), shown once.
- **Password hashing:** bcrypt cost ≥12 is the proven baseline both panels use; **argon2id** (time=3, mem=64MiB, parallelism=1) is the stronger modern default for a new panel. Constant-time compare.
- **Brute-force throttle:** per-IP + per-username (3x-ui: 5 failures / 5-min window / 15-min lockout). Log every failed/blocked login with IP + reason; opt-in Telegram/webhook alert. ZetaVPN's `config.py` already has `login_max_attempts=8`, `login_lockout_seconds=300`.
- **TOTP 2FA:** standard (6 digits, 30s, SHA1), stored encrypted, safely disabled on credential change. (`User.totp_secret/totp_enabled` present.)
- **HTTPS effectively mandatory:** built-in ACME path + direct cert/key config; HSTS `max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` when serving TLS directly, with a skip flag behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy (`XUI_SKIP_HSTS` analog).
- **Browser-hardening headers on every response:** `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `Referrer-Policy: no-referrer`, strict **nonce-based CSP** (`object-src 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'`). **CSRF** per-session random token, constant-time compare, `X-CSRF-Token`; exempt only bearer-token API callers.
- **Least-exposed network posture:** offer binding panel to `127.0.0.1` behind a reverse proxy; **CORS same-origin by default** — do **not** ship `cors_origins="*"` in production (the current `config.py` default is `"*"` — tighten before release). Disable API docs/Swagger + debug by default; enable via explicit opt-in.
- **RBAC:** separate super-admin from scoped operators (Marzban's sudo vs admin, 403 on privileged endpoints) so a compromised operator can't alter system settings, import DBs, or push dangerous config. (`User.role` field present.)
- **Secrets at rest:** JWT key, TOTP secrets, DB DSN passwords, node mTLS keys, Telegram tokens at 0600 with a dedicated owner; keep private keys out of any settings export/UI; redact in logs and the API (return `has_X` booleans, not values). DB file (`data/zeta.db`) 0600.

---

## 9. RECOMMENDED ZETAVPN ARCHITECTURE

This section makes concrete, justified decisions. The existing `F:\ZetaVPN\backend` codebase already implements most of them correctly; deltas from current state are flagged.

### 9.1 Backend language & stack — **Python 3.11+ / FastAPI / SQLAlchemy 2.0 / Pydantic v2 / Uvicorn**

**Decision:** keep the existing FastAPI stack (`F:\ZetaVPN\backend\zeta`). **Justification:** the two mature reference panels are split between Go (3x-ui, S-UI) and Python (Marzban, Marzneshin, Hiddify); Python/FastAPI matches Marzban exactly, which means its serving patterns, subscription routes, psutil stats, and Jinja2 templates are directly reusable. The dossiers confirm the codebase is already built this way and idiomatic. A Go rewrite would buy a single-binary embed at the cost of throwing away a working, correct implementation — not justified for a single-maintainer personal panel. Config via `pydantic-settings` with `ZETA_` prefix + `.env` (already done).

### 9.2 Database — **SQLite (WAL) via SQLAlchemy, Postgres-ready**

**Decision:** SQLite by default at `data/zeta.db`; keep the SQLAlchemy layer DSN-swappable to Postgres later. **Justification:** every single-node panel (3x-ui, S-UI, Marzban's default) uses SQLite; it needs zero setup, is trivial to back up (single file) and to bundle in the installer. The normalized schema (`inbounds` + `clients` FK + `ssh_accounts` + `settings` KV + `users` + `traffic_snapshots` + `audit_logs`) already exists in `models.py` and is *better* than 3x-ui's classic denormalized "clients-as-JSON-inside-inbound" model — easier per-client traffic/expiry queries. Store the core-facing `settings`/`stream_settings` as **raw JSON columns** passed verbatim to the core (keeps ZetaVPN protocol-agnostic and forward-compatible — cores evolve faster than any schema). *Delta:* provide a converter that reads 3x-ui `inbounds.settings` JSON for migration compatibility.

### 9.3 Front end — **no-npm-build: FastAPI static HTML + vanilla JS**

**Decision:** keep the existing hand-written static HTML + framework-free vanilla JS served by FastAPI (`StaticFiles` + `FileResponse`); optional **CDN-vendored-locally Alpine.js (~15KB)** + a hand-written/prebuilt CSS; **qrcodejs vendored locally**; **Canvas 2D** throughput chart (no chart lib). **Justification:** both reference SPAs require a full Node build (3x-ui: React 19 + AntD 6 + **Vite 8, Node ≥22**; Marzban: React 18 + Chakra + Vite 3). That directly conflicts with ZetaVPN's "auto-install from GitHub, no Node build step" goal and burdens a single maintainer. The existing stack (`frontend/assets/js/app.js` with `h()`/`modal()`/`every()`/`drawChart()`, `api.js` Bearer wrapper, `main.py` mounting `{BASE}/assets` and serving `index.html`/`sub.html`) already mirrors the reference UX (sidebar, stat cards, throughput chart, modals, polling) with **zero toolchain**. The installer just copies `frontend/` next to the backend. Alpine.js optionally replaces XSS-prone `innerHTML` string-templating. **Do NOT** use the Tailwind Play CDN in production (runtime-compiled, slow) — ship a prebuilt/hand-written sheet. Vendor **all** client libs locally so installs are offline-capable with no third-party CSP/privacy concern on the login page.

### 9.4 Core binaries — **two cores: Xray-core + sing-box, as separate systemd services**

**Decision:** run **both** cores, each as its own systemd unit with its own JSON config on non-overlapping ports; the DB records `core = 'xray' | 'singbox'` per inbound (already in `models.py`). **Justification:** no single core covers the protocol matrix. Xray owns VLESS-REALITY/XTLS-Vision + widest client compatibility + **live gRPC HandlerService** (add/remove users without restart — avoids dropping all connections). sing-box owns Hysteria2/TUIC/ShadowTLS/AnyTLS/Naive + modern QUIC/BBR. Both are single Go binaries auto-downloadable from GitHub releases. The panel writes each config, validates (`xray run -test` / `sing-box check -c`), then restarts **only** the affected service. Wire an `api` inbound + routing rule to `127.0.0.1:62789` (ZetaVPN's chosen Xray gRPC port) into every generated Xray config so StatsService/HandlerService work. For sing-box (no live handler), regenerate + reload on change, batching, accepting the brief reconnect. Native SSH stack is the third pillar, driven by shell-outs from `ssh_manager.py`.

### 9.5 Directory layout

```
/opt/zetavpn/                      # ZETA_HOME
├── zeta/                          # FastAPI backend package
│   ├── main.py  config.py  db.py  models.py  schemas.py  auth.py  deps.py  tasks.py  bootstrap.py
│   ├── api/      auth clients inbounds settings ssh subscription system
│   └── core/     xray singbox protocols links services ssh_manager system_stats
├── frontend/assets/{css,js}       # static, no build; index.html sub.html login
├── data/zeta.db                   # SQLite (0600)
├── .env                           # generated secrets/creds (0600)
└── install.sh update.sh
/usr/local/bin/{xray,sing-box}
/usr/local/etc/xray/config.json    # generated
/etc/sing-box/config.json          # generated
/etc/systemd/system/{zeta-panel,zeta-xray,zeta-singbox}.service
/var/log/zetavpn/
```

### 9.6 Services (systemd)

`zeta-panel` (Uvicorn, non-root `zetavpn` user, hardened) · `zeta-xray` · `zeta-singbox` · SSH-stack units (`zeta-ws`, `dropbear`, `stunnel4`, `dnstt`, `badvpn`, `wg-quick@wg0`, `openvpn-server@*`) · a `zeta-reaper` timer (expiry/quota sweep + SSH-account reconcile) · acme.sh cron.

### 9.7 Prioritized feature checklist

**MVP (v1) — ship these:**
- Two-core config generation from DB (Xray + sing-box), validate-then-reload; `api` inbound + stats routing wired.
- Curated inbound templates: **VLESS-REALITY (default one-click)**, VLESS-WS-TLS (CDN), Trojan-WS-TLS, Shadowsocks-2022, Hysteria2, TUIC.
- Key/cred provisioning by shelling to core CLIs: `xray uuid`/`xray x25519`/`xray vlessenc`, `sing-box generate uuid`/`reality-keypair`/`rand --hex 8`, `openssl rand -base64 {16,32}` (validate SS2022 PSK length).
- Per-client quota + expiry + enable/disable + IP-limit; live online status; live add/remove via Xray HandlerService; StatsService poll every 30s.
- User-centric subscription: `/sub/{token}` (base64) + **`/clash` + `/sing-box`** aggregate outputs via User-Agent sniff (+ `?target=` override); `Subscription-Userinfo` header; per-config QR + subscription-URL QR.
- SSH stack: OpenSSH + Dropbear + stunnel + SSH-WS + BadVPN + SlowDNS + OpenVPN + WireGuard, Linux-user account model, DB-reconciled expiry, autokill concurrent-login enforcement.
- Web UI: dashboard (psutil stats + Canvas throughput), inbound/client tables (used/total progress bar, expiry countdown), add-client modal, share-link/QR export, user portal page, dark theme default + responsive/mobile sidebar, Swagger opt-in.
- Auth/security: session-or-JWT login, argon2id/bcrypt, login throttle, TOTP 2FA, CSRF, hardening headers, secret URL base path, RBAC (admin/reseller).
- Installer: `curl|bash`, arch/distro detect, verified download, systemd non-root units, acme.sh, BBR+sysctl, ufw+fail2ban+unattended-upgrades, non-interactive mode, `zeta update` self-update with rollback, 3x-ui DB importer.

**Later (post-MVP) — deliberately deferred:**
- Multi-node gRPC control plane (`Node` table stubbed now, agents built later) · reseller/multi-admin hierarchies beyond basic RBAC · Telegram bot (same headless API) · periodic traffic-reset scheduling beyond expiry+quota · WARP/CDN auto-config, decoy/home pages, DoH · MTProto (`mtg` sidecar) · ShadowTLS/AnyTLS/Naive as first-class UI templates (config plumbing ready via sing-box) · Postgres backend · Hiddify-specific JSON profile output.

**Justification for the cuts:** these are the Hiddify/Marzban enterprise-flavored features that add the largest maintenance surface (the gRPC node plane alone is the single biggest complexity source in the entire landscape) for zero benefit on a personal single-node build. Keeping only cheap, expected features maximizes shipping velocity for one maintainer.

---

- https://github.com/2dust/v2rayN · https://github.com/2dust/v2rayN/blob/master/v2rayN/ServiceLib/Handler/SubscriptionHandler.cs · https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/2dust/v2rayN@7.23.1/v2rayN/ServiceLib/Handler/Fmt/{BaseFmt,VmessFmt,VLESSFmt,TrojanFmt,ShadowsocksFmt}.cs

**UI/UX**
- https://alpinejs.dev/start-here · https://tailwindcss.com/docs/installation/play-cdn

**Existing ZetaVPN codebase (verified this session):** `F:\ZetaVPN\backend\zeta\{config.py, models.py, main.py, core/links.py, core/xray.py, core/singbox.py, core/ssh_manager.py, core/system_stats.py, api/subscription.py}`
